sources.name: GDELT
sources.notes: Use for topic detection, clustering, geography hints, and media-attention bursts. Do not use as the sole basis for a final bet.
sources.license_notes: Free/open platform; verify reuse terms per downstream workflow.

sources.name: World Bank Indicators API
sources.notes: Use for structural baselines and country comparisons.
sources.license_notes: Open World Bank data; confirm per-series usage terms if redistributed.

sources.name: ILOSTAT Bulk Download Facility
sources.notes: Use the official bulk indicator CSV downloads for labour structure, participation, unemployment, sector dynamics, and informality.
sources.license_notes: Public bulk download access exposed by ILOSTAT; verify per-dataset reuse terms if redistributed.

sources.name: USGS Earthquake Feeds
sources.notes: Use for precise hazard confirmation with timestamps and magnitude.
sources.license_notes: Machine-readable feeds exposed publicly by USGS.

sources.name: OpenStreetMap Overpass API
sources.update_cadence: Near real time with some lag
sources.notes: Use for infrastructure density, place context, proximity checks, and asset lookup.
sources.license_notes: Read-only API over OSM data; respect OSM/instance usage expectations.

sources.name: EIA API
sources.coverage: Energy markets, electricity, fuels, and system data
sources.notes: Use for energy system context and supply-demand stress.
sources.license_notes: Free API, but requires an API key.

signal_convergences.convergence_title: Treating urban water stress as a temporary inconvenience

future_bets.title: Treating urban water stress as a temporary inconvenience
future_bets.summary: Direct groundwater depletion reporting, reinforcing heat-stress coverage, and continuing urban growth suggest that urban water stress in India is becoming a more structural operating constraint.
future_bets.implication: Teams deciding where to build, manufacture, or expand should treat water resilience and cooling resilience as real site-selection variables.
future_bets.why_now: A direct urban groundwater warning matters more when near-term heat pressure and long-term urban demand are still rising together.

second_order_effects: Water monitoring, reuse, and efficiency products become more relevant.
Location strategy may shift toward more resilient corridors.
what_to_watch: Repeated local water-shortage reporting and rationing events.
Urban growth and infrastructure investment trends.
what_could_change_this: Large improvements in water infrastructure and reuse could reduce stress.
A run of milder seasons could soften near-term pressure.
